aliases: how past lessons reach the prompt
blurb: How lessons reach the prompt: the UserPromptSubmit hook, the loopback recall borrow, and the long-lived recall/tree-nav MCP server.
intent: Show how recalled memories reach an agent and why the delivery path is shaped the way it is. The `UserPromptSubmit` handler is a fresh short-lived process per prompt, so it borrows the warm `regin serve` embedder via a loopback-auth-exempt POST to `/api/memory/recall` (`_recall_via_server`) and falls back to in-process FTS-only (`_recall_fts`); it routes the prompt to an authoritative topic and can withhold a `<topic_context>` banner. Speculative injection never reinforces — overlap-gated, uncalibrated-capped, same-session deduped via `injection_events` — except the earned `reinforce_resurfaced`; each inject and routed banner is recorded and `_emit_recall_span` writes a `memory.recall` span. `api_memory_recall` computes route-time topic suppression (it needs the warm embedder) and returns hits plus `topic_suppress`. The long-lived `recall` MCP server exposes `recall`, the address-only tree-nav tools `index_root`/`index_expand`/`index_fetch`, and a `gate` tool for trace-derived span gates.
label: Memory auto-injection & on-demand recall (UserPromptSubmit hook, recall MCP server, /api/memory/recall)
